description: Recommended MCP server registry for the game-development scaffold. This file is intentionally configuration-oriented and uses placeholder commands or environment keys where local setup differs by team.

filesystem.purpose: Read and traverse the working repository, generated docs, logs, and local output files.
when_to_use: read generated design or technical docs
notes: Restrict access to the project root or approved workspace directories.
Prefer read-first workflows; write access should be intentional.

git.purpose: Inspect branches, diffs, blame, recent changes, and release history.
when_to_use: prepare release notes or patch notes
notes: Keep repository scope explicit.
Use together with filesystem access for code and content reviews.

github.purpose: Review pull requests, issues, discussions, and remote repository state.
when_to_use: inspect CI status or release metadata
notes: Use least-privilege tokens.
Useful for PR review, release planning, and issue triage agents.

ripgrep.purpose: Fast codebase and content text search for symbols, config keys, and repeated patterns.
notes: Best paired with filesystem and git servers.
Critical for large Unity, Unreal, and Godot repositories.

fetch-docs.purpose: Retrieve official documentation pages, package docs, API references, and public technical references.
notes: Prefer official docs domains in production workflows.
Useful for all engine-specific reviewers and build resolvers.

unity-docs.purpose: Unity scripting, manual, package, and platform-build documentation.
notes: Use for Unity-only sessions and profiles.
Keep package-version context explicit in docs and technical design.

unity-package-registry.purpose: Lookup Unity package metadata, versions, changelogs, and dependency notes.
notes: Useful for build engineers, reviewers, and package governance workflows.

unreal-docs.purpose: Unreal documentation for framework classes, packaging, rendering, networking, and editor workflows.
notes: Use for Unreal-only sessions and profile installs.

godot-docs.purpose: Godot class reference, export docs, scene/node docs, and editor workflow guidance.
notes: Use for Godot-only sessions and profile installs.

issue-tracker.purpose: Read and update backlog items, bugs, milestones, and release tasks in the team issue tracker.
notes: Recommended for producer, QA, release, and doc-updater workflows.
Keep write permissions intentional.

telemetry.purpose: Inspect dashboards, KPI tables, event definitions, and post-launch metrics.
notes: Use for telemetry analysts, liveops managers, and mobile F2P reviews.
Avoid exposing unnecessary player-level data.

fal-media.purpose: Interactive AI asset generation via the fal.ai model catalog: images, skyboxes, 3D models, sound effects, music, speech, and video.
when_to_use: explore or compare generation models before committing them to the provider registry
iterate interactively on prompts before a reproducible generation run
estimate generation cost before batch work
notes: Optional convenience layer. Reproducible production generation goes through scripts/generate-assets.js and manifests/asset-providers.json so provenance sidecars are written.
Follow the AI-generated asset rules in rules/common/asset-pipeline.md regardless of which path generated the file.

playwright.purpose: Drive a real browser to playtest and QA web (HTML5) builds: navigate, click/drag/type, capture screenshots, and read console + page errors.
when_to_use: smoke-test a running web build end to end (boot, start, play, restart)
capture gameplay or menu screenshots for review
verify menu/HUD flows and surface console or page errors
check GPU-memory-flat or perf signals exposed on window during play
notes: Microsoft's official Playwright MCP. First run needs browsers installed: `npx playwright install chromium`.
Point it at a local dev/preview server (e.g. `npm run dev` / `npm run preview`), not a file:// path, so ES modules load.
Optional interactive layer. Each sample still keeps a reproducible smoke path in its own scripts/ (headless sim test + a committed Playwright script); the MCP is for live, ad hoc QA.
Web-target only — it tests browser builds, not the Unity/Unreal/Godot editors (use an engine MCP for those).

storefront-docs.purpose: Review store submission rules, metadata requirements, release checklists, and platform documentation.
notes: Useful for release-manager and console-compliance-reviewer workflows.

ci-builds.purpose: Inspect CI pipelines, build artifacts, test outputs, and packaging logs.
notes: Recommended for build engineers, release managers, and engine build resolvers.
